7:15PM: Sighted Point Indio Light Ship dead ahead. 7PM: This page (in Spanish, but translated by Google) gives a history of the Point Indio light ships and indicates that the ship that was there in 1848 was at location 35 deg S, 57 deg 3' W.  http://www.histarmar.com.ar/InfHistorica-4/Practicos/PontonesRdlP.htm

7:45AM: let go anchor Bearings at anchorage.  Quilmers Tower bore SSW3/4W.  Custom House W1/4S pc 7AM: I'm guessing that Quilmers is Quilmes, a city to the south of Buenos Aires.  I can't any references to a tower there, so I'm guessing that it might have been the cathedral, Catedral De Quilmes; https://www.google.com/maps/place/Catedral+De+Quilmes/@-34.7206538,-58.2542834,15z/data=!4m6!3m5!1s0x95a32e408604da8b:0x9ba04254528a6588!8m2!3d-34.7206538!4d-58.2542834!16s%2Fg%2F11c1rsb025?entry=ttu and discussed here, https://www.adequatetravel.com/placeguide/Argentina/catedral-de-quilmes-in-argentina-history-facts-services 7AM: The current Customs House was built in 1910.  The previous Customs House was built in 1855 and through annexations over the years became the primary government administrative building, Casa Rosada; http://www.geonames.org/6285782/Casa%20Rosada.html  Here's a site that discusses its history, https://www.casarosada.gob.ar/international/argentine-government-house/history

Other: 9:30AM: H.B.M.S Garnet made signal, !Hostilities has ceased for twelve hours! from last night which gives hopes of peace, but all hands below 60 are called to arms.

Note: 6AM: The 6 AM wet bulb temperature was recorded as 47, but crossed out.  The dry bulb temperature at that time was recorded as 46.  So it must have been noticed that the value was higher than the dry bulb temperature and deemed to likely be invalid.  I did not transcribe the value.

Other: Sunrise: dressed ship with the US Ensign at the fore.  Main and peak and Jack forward in celebration of the anniversary of the 4th July 1776.
Noon: fired a salute of 21 guns in honor of the anniversary of American independence, and H.M.S Garnet and German Corvette !Ariadne! did the same.

12:05AM: Steam Cutter parted the hawser at the boom and drifted astern and then riding by the stern hawser.  Got a guy on the hawser so as to have the cutter ride directly astern in the smoother water, but being so water logged and risking life to order men in to bail her out.  She continued to ship the heavy seas and at about 12.15 she entirely filled and sank.  Double bouyed the hawser.  Ship heading at 12.15 ExS Bearing of the cutter as she disappeared WxN1/2N distant from ship about ten (10) fathoms.  Marion bore from Cutter when sinking W1/2N.  Depth of water five (5) fathoms.  First bouy was placed on the hawser about ten (10) fathoms from cutter and the second on the end of the hawser.
4 to 8PM: Engaged with all hands in raising the Steam Cutter. Recovered the Steam Cutter
8PM to Mid: Hoisted Steam Cutter and hove in to 105 fathoms on bower chain.
